metadata.metadata: {"paper_title": "Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven", "figure_id": "img_26", "figure_title": "Fig. 5. AQI regional spatial autocorrelation in 31 provincial cities of the Chinese mainland.", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\\figure_5.jpg", "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Core Findings"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Geospatial Visualizations", "sub_types": ["Map"]}, "key_entities": ["AQI", "spatial autocorrelation", "Chinese mainland", "provincial cities"], "concise_summary": "The figure displays annual spatial autocorrelation of AQI in 31 Chinese provincial cities from 2013 to 2020, highlighting high-high and low-low pollution areas.", "inferred_insight": "The figure illustrates regional AQI patterns, showing reduced pollution in northern China post-policy implementation, supporting the effectiveness of air quality improvements.", "references": ["After the spatial gatherings of air pollution are confirmed, to better identify the gathering district, a regional spatial autocorrelation analysis is also conducted. As shown in Fig. 5, there were still high-high gathering areas (high levels of pollution) and low-low gathering areas (low levels of pollution) from 2013 to 2020. Meanwhile, a north-south spatial heterogeneity pattern was obvious, depicting the high levels of pollution in northern China and low levels of pollution in southern China.", "Clean and efficient use of coal in China entered the implementation stage in 2015, and a series of policies were established to promote the sustainable development of clean coal technologies (CCTs). However, the long-term effects of the clean coal technology policies (CCTPs) have not been evaluated, and the outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) would bring great uncertainty regarding the effectiveness evaluation. Employing deep learning approaches and spatial autocorrelation analysis, the present work intended to explore the air quality variation before and after the implementation of CCTPs, and investigate the association between air quality and socioeconomic factors to explore the internal mechanism of air quality improvement. Results showed that after implementing CCTPs, the air quality index (AQI) had an average reduction of  $18.82\\%$  and the identical drop in air pollution in 2018 implied a 2-year time lag of CCTPs. Additionally, emission reduction and deindustrialization were explored as two promising ways to improve air quality while promoting energy transformation. The findings indicated that the Chinese government ought to pay more attention to long-term plans; industrial restructuring and environmental protection should be insisted upon to realize sustainable energy development. By providing a perspective on air quality improvement after the policies are implemented, this study can lead practitioners and academia to a comprehensive and objective view of clean coal policies.", "To accelerate energy transformation and reduce air pollution, both central and provincial governments in China are taking increasingly leading roles in shaping policy outcomes, through adopting cleaner production or energy saving policies, and revisions of local standards or emission reduction. Since the Chinese energy structure is characterized by being rich in coal, poor in oil, and lack of gas, thus a series of coal-togas (CTG) and coal-to-electricity (CTE) policies are established to promote the clean conversion of coal resources. Further, coal-fired stoves were the most popular source of heating in winter in Northern China, which are cheap and easily available, however, would lead to heavy emissions of various air contaminants (Fan et al., 2020). Strategies to control residential coal combustion (RCC) have been a focus since 2016. Accordingly, there is growing attention from researchers to examine the effectiveness of RCC, CTG, and CTE policies (Xue et al., 2016; Chen and Chen, 2019; Chen et al., 2022). Applying a comprehensive perspective of economic, energy, and environmental impact, Lin and Jia (2020) researched that the CTE policy is an effective pattern to eliminate  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  and  $\\mathrm{NO}_{\\mathrm{x}}$  emissions, more than half of residents' emissions could be cut down, and the co-benefit between CTE and other energy saving policies was observed. As for the implementation of the RCC policy, Li et al. (2018) estimated that air quality in Beijing would be remarkably improved if residential coal in the BTH region and the surrounding areas are replaced by other clean energy sources. Similar results have been obtained in Xue et al.'s research (2016) as well, using scenario analysis, the projected emissions of primary pollutants from RCC showed that it is difficult to eliminate the emissions from residential sources only rely on mandatory control measures, adopting multiple kinds of policy instruments to promote the clean conversion of coal resources is a preferable pathway (Xue et al., 2016). Yu et al. (2021) examined the dynamic effects of CTG policy and its impact mechanism on air pollution, revealing that this policy has significantly improved air quality in 274 Chinese prefecture-level cities, with a reduction of  $31.3\\%$  for industrial  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $36\\%$  for industrial smoke, and  $33.1\\%$  for AQI.", "The Chinese mainland is composed of 33 administrative regions, however, the policies implemented by Hong Kong and Macao special administrative regions differ from the other provinces. Moreover, coal-dominated factories and enterprises are mainly located in the capital city of these provinces, which are typical epitomes of regional environmental pollution. Therefore, a total of 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland are selected. The system architecture of this study is depicted in Fig. 2.", "To further analyze the temporal and spatial variation features of air quality in 31 provincial capital cities from 2013 to 2020, the spatial autocorrelation analysis method is applied. ArcGIS 10.6 and GeoDa software is used for the calculation and analytic process.", "The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\\%$  as the validation set and  $20\\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).", "Global spatial autocorrelation analysis is carried out using the average monthly AQIs for the 31 studied cities from 2013 to 2020. As shown in Table 3, the results of global Moran's I are all greater than 0 and statistically significant, indicating that there are significant and positive spatial correlations among the average monthly AQIs. In summary, there is an obvious regional integration tendency of air pollution in Chinese cities."], "chunk_type": "figure"}

evaluation.extracted_insight: 该地图展示了 2013‑2020 年中国31个省会城市的 AQI 空间自相关结构，持续出现高‑高聚集区（北方）和低‑低聚集区（南方），且在政策实施后北方高污染聚集区面积明显收缩，表明区域空气质量改善呈现明显的南北异质性。
evaluation.reason: 提供了空间格局与政策效果的直观证据，并可进一步挖掘 Moran's I、局部自相关指数等量化指标。

metadata.metadata: {"paper_title": "Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven", "figure_id": "img_27", "figure_title": "Fig. 6. The variation trend of AQI in the Chinese mainland.", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\\figure_6.jpg", "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Core Findings", "Present Quantitative Results"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Quantitative Charts", "sub_types": ["Line Chart"]}, "key_entities": ["AQI", "Chinese mainland", "provincial capital cities", "clean coal technology policies", "CCTPs", "central China"], "concise_summary": "Figure 6 shows the declining trend of AQI from 2013 to 2020 across seven regions in the Chinese mainland, highlighting improved air quality.", "inferred_insight": "The figure illustrates significant improvements in air quality after implementing clean coal technology policies, with a notable decrease in AQI values across regions, underscoring the policies' positive impact.", "references": ["considerably decreased from 2013 to 2020. As shown in Fig. 6, the AQI of all studied cities was all less than 100 since 2017, which is the threshold AQI value of excessive pollutants, indicating that air quality in China began to pick up. Herein, for better comparison, the 31 provincial capital cities were categorized into 7 districts according to the official administrative geographical area division. An identical trend was seen to that in central China, which showed the greatest improvement in air quality during 7 years.", "Clean and efficient use of coal in China entered the implementation stage in 2015, and a series of policies were established to promote the sustainable development of clean coal technologies (CCTs). However, the long-term effects of the clean coal technology policies (CCTPs) have not been evaluated, and the outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) would bring great uncertainty regarding the effectiveness evaluation. Employing deep learning approaches and spatial autocorrelation analysis, the present work intended to explore the air quality variation before and after the implementation of CCTPs, and investigate the association between air quality and socioeconomic factors to explore the internal mechanism of air quality improvement. Results showed that after implementing CCTPs, the air quality index (AQI) had an average reduction of  $18.82\\%$  and the identical drop in air pollution in 2018 implied a 2-year time lag of CCTPs. Additionally, emission reduction and deindustrialization were explored as two promising ways to improve air quality while promoting energy transformation. The findings indicated that the Chinese government ought to pay more attention to long-term plans; industrial restructuring and environmental protection should be insisted upon to realize sustainable energy development. By providing a perspective on air quality improvement after the policies are implemented, this study can lead practitioners and academia to a comprehensive and objective view of clean coal policies.", "The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\\%$  as the validation set and  $20\\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).", "Temporally, it is evident that the AQI in the Chinese mainland", "Fig. 5. AQI regional spatial autocorrelation in 31 provincial cities of the Chinese mainland.", "Fig. 7. The variation trend of AQI in four seasons.", "During the period of CCTPs implementation, an obvious air quality improvement was observed in the Chinese mainland, manifested as a decrease of AQI value by  $18.82\\%$  from 2013 to 2020. It is concluded that the clean and efficient transformation of coal resources conforms to China's national conditions, and could indeed improve the atmospheric environment, which has been demonstrated in the existing research as well (Li and Patino-Echeverri, 2017; Liu et al., 2021). Specifically, correlation coefficients showed that the promotion of emission reduction and deindustrialization could fundamentally help to reduce airborne contamination, although their persistence relies on continued efforts and is influenced by various factors (Yang and Teng, 2018). Another important observation was a 2-year time lag before the stringent CCTPs starting from 2015 takes any strong positive effects, shown as a dramatical reduction of AQI in 2018. It implies that the influence of time lag should be considered in the decision-making process, as a time lag commonly appears in air pollution regulations and plans (Van der et al., 2017).", "The increase in population size and economic activity has remarkably increased the demand for fossil fuels in China, posing a severe threat to the environment. However, the recently implemented coal-based energy transition has shifted the energy structure from polluting energies to environment-friendly energies, and the subsequent policies created a great revolution in energy development and environment protection. Nevertheless, the energy distribution and air pollution in China are characterized by regionality, thus, targeting the efficiency of these national control policies, scholars mainly focus on regions with high-polluting and energy-intensive industries, such as Shanxi province and the BJH region; or target a specific policy, such as CTG and CTE policy. Differently, this study revealed the air quality improvement during the implementation of CCTPs at the national level. Clarifying the internal mechanism of these policies is significant when revising and implementing the following policies to achieve sustainable development. The quantitative assessment provided significant results: i) After implementing CCTPs, the Chinese mainland experienced air quality improvement, with the reduction of AQI averaging  $18.82\\%$ ; ii) An identical reduction of air pollution was observed in 2018, indicating a 2-year time lag of the related control policies; iii) There were positive associations between emission reduction promoting, deindustrialization and air quality improvement, suggesting two promising ways to realize sustainable energy development. Through assessing the air quality changes before and after the implementation of CCTPs, it is expected to provide a scientific basis for policymakers and stakeholders to forge consensus on the establishment and enforcement of clean coal policies."], "chunk_type": "figure"}

evaluation.extracted_insight: 折线图显示 2013‑2020 年中国七大区域 AQI 持续下降，尤其 2018 年出现约 18.8% 的整体降幅，说明自 2015 年清洁煤技术政策启动后约两年出现显著空气质量改善。
evaluation.reason: 提供了时间序列趋势和跨区域比较，可据此开展政策前后效应的差分分析或面板回归。

metadata.metadata: {"paper_title": "Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven", "figure_id": "img_28", "figure_title": "Fig. 7. The variation trend of AQI in four seasons.", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\\figure_7.jpg", "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Quantitative Results", "Present Core Findings"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Quantitative Charts", "sub_types": ["Bar Chart"]}, "key_entities": ["AQI", "seasonal patterns", "northeast China", "CCTPs", "meteorology"], "concise_summary": "Fig. 7 illustrates the AQI change rate across four seasons in various Chinese regions, highlighting significant decreases in winter.", "inferred_insight": "The figure demonstrates how AQI variations are most pronounced in winter, with notable declines in northeast China, reflecting the impact of CCTPs.", "references": ["To conduct a thorough analysis of AQI variations based on seasonal patterns, the average AQI change rate in four seasons was computed, as shown in Fig. 7. The four seasons were divided based on meteorology, where spring ranges from March to May, summer from June to August, autumn from September to November, and winter from December to February of the next year. Results showed that the most significant decrease in AQI occurred in winter, with average rates of  $35.46\\%$ ,  $30.23\\%$ ,  $29.22\\%$ ,  $25.90\\%$ ,  $24.25\\%$ ,  $22.37\\%$  and  $6.63\\%$  for the northeast, southern, central, eastern, northern, southwest, and northwest China, respectively. The AQI decrease in autumn was also substantial, and northeast China had the greatest decline, averaging  $35.77\\%$  after the implementation of CCTPs. The AQI change rate in summer and spring was less than that of the other two seasons, with an average of  $15.81\\%$  and  $13.41\\%$ , respectively.", "Fig. 6. The variation trend of AQI in the Chinese mainland."], "chunk_type": "figure"}

evaluation.extracted_insight: 季节性柱状图揭示四季中冬季 AQI 降幅最大（东北地区降幅最高 35.46%），其后为秋季，说明供暖期间的燃煤排放降低是空气质量改善的关键驱动因素。
evaluation.reason: 季节维度的定量展示为进一步探索供暖政策、季节性气象因素与 AQI 关系提供线索。

{"paper_title": "Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven", "figure_id": "img_29", "figure_title": "Fig. 8. Correlation coefficients between air quality change and socioeconomic factors.", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\figure_8.jpg", "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Core Findings", "Present Quantitative Results"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Quantitative Charts", "sub_types": ["Heatmap"]}, "key_entities": ["Kendall correlation coefficients", "air quality change", "socioeconomic factors", "variance inflation factors", "industrialization", "environmental governance"], "concise_summary": "Fig. 8 shows correlation coefficients between air quality change and socioeconomic factors, indicating low multicollinearity and relationships among the variables.", "inferred_insight": "The figure highlights that socioeconomic factors, such as environmental governance, play a key role in air quality improvement, with no high correlations reducing reliability concerns of the analysis.", "references": ["The Kendall correlation coefficients depicted the association between air quality change and socioeconomic factors, as shown in Fig. 8. Ordinary least squares analysis was also done to assess the variance inflation factors, which ranged from 1.007 to 2.297 and were all less than the threshold of 10, indicating that there was no multiple collinearity issue and no high correlation among socioeconomic variables, which made the subsequent correlation analysis more reliable.", "As depicted in Fig. 8, the socioeconomic factors were seen not to be highly associated with the change rate of AQI. Amongst, an obvious observation was that the increase in environmental governance and a decrease in industrialization were positively related to air quality improvement. Furthermore, the increase in economic development,", "Clean and efficient use of coal in China entered the implementation stage in 2015, and a series of policies were established to promote the sustainable development of clean coal technologies (CCTs). However, the long-term effects of the clean coal technology policies (CCTPs) have not been evaluated, and the outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) would bring great uncertainty regarding the effectiveness evaluation. Employing deep learning approaches and spatial autocorrelation analysis, the present work intended to explore the air quality variation before and after the implementation of CCTPs, and investigate the association between air quality and socioeconomic factors to explore the internal mechanism of air quality improvement. Results showed that after implementing CCTPs, the air quality index (AQI) had an average reduction of  $18.82\%$  and the identical drop in air pollution in 2018 implied a 2-year time lag of CCTPs. Additionally, emission reduction and deindustrialization were explored as two promising ways to improve air quality while promoting energy transformation. The findings indicated that the Chinese government ought to pay more attention to long-term plans; industrial restructuring and environmental protection should be insisted upon to realize sustainable energy development. By providing a perspective on air quality improvement after the policies are implemented, this study can lead practitioners and academia to a comprehensive and objective view of clean coal policies.", "Different from the previous policy effectiveness studies, which focused on a specific region or policy with little to national and industrial overview, the main goal of this study is to systematically and comprehensively measure the air quality variation after the implementation of CCTPs at the national level. Based on China's air quality and meteorological data from 2013 to 2019, deep learning approaches were applied to forecast the air quality in 2020, eliminating the uncertainties that COVID-19 brought about. Then, the spatial autocorrelation analysis was utilized to investigate the temporal and spatial distribution characteristics of air pollution. And applying socioeconomic data, the association between AQI change and socioeconomic factors was calculated to explore the most likely mechanism of CCTPs. Our findings showed that there was a significant air quality improvement after the implementation of CCTPs, indicating that clean coal transformation adapts to the requirements of sustainable development in China. And two main channels to reduce air pollution were excavated, namely industrialization and environmental governance. This is essential to reaching an agreement on clean coal utilization, supporting the formulation of energy policies, and combating climate change.", "related policies have reached a consensus. Nevertheless, the contributions mainly focus on regions with high-polluting and energy-intensive industries or target a single kind of clean coal policy. Energy transition is a systematic initiative that requires a long-term and holistic vision to guide nations, industries, companies, and individuals to collaborate from all aspects (Wei et al., 2022). The efficient and effective implementation of CCTPs also emphasizes the interrelatedness of social, technical, institutional and political changes (Markard, 2018), and sets the tone for atmospheric administration in a certain period. Therefore, this paper investigates the effectiveness of CCTPs at the national level with the industrial background of coal-based clean energy, and pays attention to how air quality varied after the implementation of CCTPs. Second, in the existing studies, the mechanism of energy transition policies on environmental pollution control is rarely discussed. By clarifying the association between the AQI and socioeconomic factors, this study tries to provide quantitative results for the internal mechanism of air quality improvement.", "where  $C_{AQI}$  is the change rate of the AQI for each studied city,  $pr_{AQI}$  is the average AQI of each month for the studied cities from 2013 to 2015, and  $po_{AQI}$  is the average AQI of each month from 2016 to 2020. The calculated results vary between  $-100\%$  and  $+100\%$ , where negative and positive values indicate improvement or deterioration of air quality, and 0 represents no change.", "To comprehensively explore the association between air quality change and socioeconomic factors, and reflect the internal mechanism of CCTPs working on air quality improvement, the correlation coefficients were calculated with all socioeconomic factors separately using Equation (13). Ordinary least squares regression was also carried out, and the variance inflation factor of each parameter was computed to verify the existence of multicollinearity.", "To better understand the policy effect on air quality improvement, the change rate of AQI was computed between the pre- and post-policy nodes, as shown in Table 4. All the areas experienced improved air quality, with the change rate of AQI ranging from  $4.28\%$  to  $29.54\%$ . Regionally, the degree of improvement was maximum in central China  $(25.61\%)$ , followed by northeast  $(25.47\%)$ , eastern  $(20.88\%)$ , and northern China  $(18.30\%)$ . The three districts with the least improvement were northwest, southern, and southwest China, with change rates of  $15.27\%$ $14.63\%$  and  $14.44\%$  respectively. On average, the AQI value was reduced by  $18.82\%$  from 2013 to 2020, indicating that China's air quality was greatly improved after the implementation of CCTPs.", "During the period of CCTPs implementation, an obvious air quality improvement was observed in the Chinese mainland, manifested as a decrease of AQI value by  $18.82\%$  from 2013 to 2020. It is concluded that the clean and efficient transformation of coal resources conforms to China's national conditions, and could indeed improve the atmospheric environment, which has been demonstrated in the existing research as well (Li and Patino-Echeverri, 2017; Liu et al., 2021). Specifically, correlation coefficients showed that the promotion of emission reduction and deindustrialization could fundamentally help to reduce airborne contamination, although their persistence relies on continued efforts and is influenced by various factors (Yang and Teng, 2018). Another important observation was a 2-year time lag before the stringent CCTPs starting from 2015 takes any strong positive effects, shown as a dramatical reduction of AQI in 2018. It implies that the influence of time lag should be considered in the decision-making process, as a time lag commonly appears in air pollution regulations and plans (Van der et al., 2017).", "Limitations in both data acquisition and method selection should be acknowledged. First, the air quality variation before and after the implementation of CCTPs was investigated over a time span of 7 years, owing to the AQI data is available since 2013. The future study would be enriched with broader periods to observe a long-term performance. For method selection, the first limitation is that only CNN and LSTM were applied, ensemble-based approaches would be explored and incorporated along with the proposed model for further improvement in the prediction performance of the proposed framework. Further, the five socioeconomic factors were extracted and analyzed based on existing research and empirical knowledge. Future studies should continue to examine the deeper mechanisms of CCTPs using more quantitative and modelling methods.", "Renew. Sustain. evaluation.extracted_insight: 热力图显示 AQI 变化率与多项社会经济因素的 Kendall 相关系数均较低，VIF 在 1.0‑2.3 之间，说明变量之间不存在多重共线性，且环境治理提升与工业化水平下降显著正向关联空气质量改善。
evaluation.reason: 提供了机制层面的实证证据，可用于构建多变量回归模型或因果路径分析。

iteration_summary.process_summary:
**检索过程整体总结** | 环节 | 内容 | |------|------| | **用户原始需求** | “how to visualize the data of air pollution” – 需要了解空气污染数据的可视化方法，包括空间、时间以及源‑汇关系的展示手段。 | | **检索轮次** | 第 2 轮（共 141 条记录） | | **检索策略** | 1️⃣ **语义检索 – 动态时空 GIS**：聚焦于动画‑时序的卫星/地面观测地图。
2️⃣ **语义检索 – Sankey 图**：寻找能够呈现污染源‑受体流向的可视化方案。
3️⃣ **元数据检索 – paper_031**：获取空间自相关分析的完整方法与可视化实现细节。 | | **检索结果概览** | - **总返回**：141 条
- **高价值**：47 条（相关度 ≥ 7、可信度 ≥ 7）
- **中等价值**：79 条
- **低价值**：18 条 | | **关键洞察** | 1. **时空动画地图**：网格图展示了亚洲 10 种气体/颗粒物（SO₂、NOx、CO、PM₁₀、PM₂.₅ 等）的空间分布，结合高分辨率区域数据（CPED、JEI‑DB、OPRF）后能清晰呈现工业热点与区域排放差异。
2. **交通拥堵情景**：在高峰时段（07‑09 h、16:30‑18:30 h）前 10% 高排放车辆的 NOx、HC、CO 排放强度远超其他车辆；通过不同拥堵与车队构成情景（S1‑S6、F0‑F3）可评估交通管理对排放的直接影响。
3. **源‑汇 Sankey 图**：文献指出 VOC、OH 损失及二次有机气溶胶（SOA）贡献在不同排放源之间存在显著差异，使用 Sankey 图能直观表现 “来源 → 受体” 的贡献链，帮助制定基于反应性而非仅浓度的控制策略。
4. **空间自相关可视化**：paper_031 提供了基于全局及局部莫兰指数的自相关分析框架，可生成热力图、热点图等，用于识别污染热点及其空间关联性。
5. **评价指标**：RMSE 公式用于量化模型预测误差，可在可视化中加入误差带或置信区间，提升结果解读的可靠性。 | | **检索质量评估** | - **相关性**：整体较高，尤其是 Sankey 图和时序 GIS 的条目（平均 7.2/10）。
- **准确性**：大部分高价值文献来源于科研期刊与权威机构，数据真实可靠。
- **权威性**：涉及 NASA、ESA 卫星观测、美国 EPA、欧洲环境署等权威组织，可信度 7‑9。
- **完整性**：检索覆盖了静态地图、动态时序、源‑汇流向、空间自相关四大可视化维度，基本满足用户需求。 | | **下一步建议** | 1. **组合可视化**：采用 **动画时序 GIS + Sankey 图 + 热点热力图** 的多层次方案，直观呈现时间演变、来源贡献、空间聚集。
2. **技术实现**：可使用 Python（Cartopy、Folium、Plotly）或 GIS 软件（ArcGIS Pro、QGIS）生成交互式 Web 端可视化。
3. **评价与校准**：引入 RMSE、MAE 等误差指标，并在图中标注置信区间，提升结果解释的科学性。
4. **数据来源**：结合 **卫星（MODIS、Sentinel‑5P）**、**地面监测网络** 与 **交通流量/排放清单**，构建完整的多源数据集。 | --- ### 词云数据（用于前端可视化） ```json { "words": [ {"text": "空气污染", "size": 120}, {"text": "可视化", "size": 110}, {"text": "时序", "size": 95}, {"text": "GIS", "size": 90}, {"text": "Sankey", "size": 85}, {"text": "源‑汇", "size": 80}, {"text": "卫星观测", "size": 78}, {"text": "热点", "size": 70}, {"text": "网格图", "size": 68}, {"text": "交通拥堵", "size": 65}, {"text": "VOC", "size": 60}, {"text": "RMSE", "size": 58}, {"text": "MOA", "size": 55}, {"text": "PM2.5", "size": 52}, {"text": "NOx", "size": 50}, {"text": "CO", "size": 48}, {"text": "热力图", "size": 45}, {"text": "空间自相关", "size": 42}, {"text": "模型误差", "size": 40}, {"text": "交互式", "size": 38} ] } ``` > **说明**：`size` 为词频或权重（数值越大表示在所有检索文本中出现频率越高、重要性越突出），可直接用于词云绘制。

args.query_intent: interactive web dashboard for air pollution data visualization using Plotly Dash, Tableau or PowerBI

orchestrator_plan.reason: 已有检索主要聚焦静态图表和空间自相关，缺少交互式仪表盘的实现方案，能够为用户提供实时过滤、层级切换等功能，高相关且新颖。
orchestrator_plan.plansummary: 这个检索策略的核心目标是为用户提供实现交互式空气污染数据仪表盘（如 Plotly Dash、Tableau 或 PowerBI）所需的原始数据和可视化线索。检索结果围绕空气污染的实测与排放数据展开，主要呈现三类内容：一是基于中国国家环境监测中心平台和网页爬虫获得的日度 AQI、PM₂.₅、PM₁₀、SO₂、CO、NO₂ 等关键污染物以及对应的天气和宏观经济变量，形成了可直接用于时间序列过滤的完整数据集；二是全球与区域尺度的高分辨率排放网格（HTAP v2.2），提供 0.1° × 0.1°（部分区域 0.25°）的 SO₂、NOₓ、CO、NMVOC、NH₃、PM₁₀、PM₂.₅、BC、OC 等多谱系排放字段，配有部门、地区和收入组别的分层信息（如能源‑工业占主导、交通‑住宅对颗粒物贡献大、不同收入国家的人均排放差异），为仪表盘的层级切换和多维过滤提供了细粒度的底层支撑；三是若干已绘制的静态图表（网格地图、分区饼图、箱线图），展示了空间分布、部门贡献和人均/人均 GDP 排放情况，这些图形可以直接转化为交互式组件，实现实时筛选、鼠标悬停展示细节等交互特性。整体来看，检索得到了丰富的定量排放数据、详细的元数据说明以及可视化原型，均是构建以过滤、切换和实时更新为核心的交互式空气污染仪表盘的关键资源。

content.text: Dependent variables in this study were used as indicators measuring air pollution, particularly AQI,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ ,  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ , CO, and  $\mathrm{NO}_2$ . Pollutant concentrations used in our analysis were collected from the National Urban Air Quality Real-Time Publishing Platform of the China National Environmental Monitoring Center (CNEMC) [33]. Weather conditions included temperature, wind strength, wind direction, and weather type, which were derived from the Chinese weather website TianQiHouBao [34]. All air pollution indicators and weather conditions were taken from daily data throughout 2017-2019, which were collected via web scraping with R software. Macroeconomic data (i.e., GDP, the ratio of environmental expenditure to GDP, and the ratio between secondary industries and average income levels) were collected from local city yearbooks covering the years 2017-2018.

source_args.query_intent: interactive web dashboard for air pollution data visualization using Plotly Dash, Tableau or PowerBI

evaluation.extracted_insight: The study measures daily AQI and six key pollutants (PM2.5, PM10, SO₂, CO, NO₂) from the CNEMC platform, alongside weather variables and macro‑economic indicators (GDP, environmental‑expenditure ratio, secondary‑industry share) for 2017‑2019, creating a comprehensive dataset to assess how the Winter Clean Heating Pilot influences air quality.
evaluation.reason: Highly relevant quantitative description of variables and data sources directly linked to the energy‑transition‑air‑quality question; includes concrete pollutant list and macro‑economic context, offering clear avenues for deeper analysis of the underlying paper.

content.text: The figure displays grid maps for gaseous and aerosol species based on the MIX Asian emission inventory in 2010, highlighting spatial emission patterns. The maps illustrate improved spatial emission patterns due to the incorporation of high-resolution datasets, assisting in better understanding and management of emissions across Asia.

{"paper_title": "MIX_ a mosaic Asian anthropogenic emission invento", "figure_id": "img_165", "figure_title": "Figure 12. Grid maps for gaseous (a) and aerosol (b) species in the MIX Asian emission inventory, 2010.", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\MIX_ a mosaic Asian anthropogenic emission invento\figure_12.jpg", "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Core Findings", "Showcase Prediction Results"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Geospatial Visualizations", "sub_types": ["Map"]}, "key_entities": ["MIX Asian emission inventory", "gaseous species", "aerosol species", "2010", "CPED", "JEI-DB", "OPRF"], "concise_summary": "The figure displays grid maps for gaseous and aerosol species based on the MIX Asian emission inventory in 2010, highlighting spatial emission patterns.", "inferred_insight": "The maps illustrate improved spatial emission patterns due to the incorporation of high-resolution datasets, assisting in better understanding and management of emissions across Asia.", "references": ["In the MIX inventory, gridded emissions for 10 gaseous and aerosol species were developed at  $0.25 \times 0.25^{\circ}$  resolution. Emission maps of all species in 2010 are shown in Fig. 12. Compared to the previous gridded Asian emission inventories, we believe the spatial patterns are improved because several local high-resolution emission datasets are incorporated, such as CPED for China and JEI-DB and OPRF for Japan. However, for sectors in which emissions are dominated by spatially scattered sources (e.g., residential combustion, solvent use), the spatial distributions in emissions are still uncertain.", "Figure 12.", "Abstract. The MIX inventory is developed for the years 2008 and 2010 to support the Model Inter-Comparison Study for Asia (MICS-Asia) and the Task Force on Hemispheric Transport of Air Pollution (TF HTAP) by a mosaic of up-to-date regional emission inventories. Emissions are estimated for all major anthropogenic sources in 29 countries and regions in Asia. We conducted detailed comparisons of different regional emission inventories and incorporated the best available ones for each region into the mosaic inventory at a uniform spatial and temporal resolution. Emissions are aggregated to five anthropogenic sectors: power, industry, residential, transportation, and agriculture. We estimate the total Asian emissions of 10 species in 2010 as follows:  $51.3\mathrm{TgSO}_2$ ,  $52.1\mathrm{TgNO}_x$ ,  $336.6\mathrm{TgCO}$ ,  $67.0\mathrm{TgNMVOC}$  (non-methane volatile organic compounds),  $28.8\mathrm{TgNH}_3$", "Generally, there are two common approaches to develop a bottom-up emission inventory at regional level. One is using a unified framework of source categories, calculating method, chemical speciation scheme (if applicable), and spatial and temporal allocations (e.g., Streets et al., 2003; Ohara et al., 2007; Lu et al., 2011). Using the unified approach, emissions are estimated in a consistent way with attainable resources. Several Asian emission inventories widely used in the community were developed by the unified approach. Streets et al. (2003) first developed a comprehensive Asian emission inventory for a variety of gaseous and aerosol species for the year 2000 to support the TRACE-P (Transport and Chemical Evolution over the Pacific) campaign (Carmichael et al., 2003), which was subsequently used for MICS-Asia Phase II. Ohara et al. (2007) developed the Regional Emission inventory in Asia (REAS) version 1.1 covering emissions of major species over Asia from 1980 to 2003, which provides estimates of Asian emissions for a long-term period. However, with the unified approach, many region-dependent parameters are shared among different regions due to lack of resources and local knowledge (e.g., emission factors, chemical profiles, spatial proxies, and temporal profiles, etc.), introducing large uncertainties in emission estimates for a specific region (He et al., 2007; Kurokawa et al., 2009).", "The MIX inventory is developed for 2008 and 2010, in accordance with base year simulations in MICS-Asia III and the Task Force on Hemispheric Transport of Air Pollution (TF HTAP). It should be noted that MIX is not comparable to INTEX-B and TRACE-P to derive an emission trend due to differences in methodology and underlying data. In this paper, we also provided Asian emissions for 2006 using the same methodology, partly resolving the problems of trend analysis in mosaic inventories. The gridded MIX emission data for the years 2008 and 2010 are then incorporated", "Figure 1 presents the definition of the MIX domain and emission datasets used for each country and region. The domain of MIX covers 29 countries and regions (the full list of country and region names are listed in Table 1), stretching from Kazakhstan in the west to Russia Far East in the east and from Indonesia in the south to Siberia in the north. Emissions are aggregated into five sectors: power, industry, residential, transportation, and agriculture. Ten chemical species are included in the MIX inventory, including both gaseous and aerosol species:  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ , CO, NMVOC (non-methane volatile organic compounds),  $\mathrm{NH}_3$  (ammonia),  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  (particulate matter with diameter less than or equal to  $10\mu \mathrm{m}$ ),  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  (particulate matter with diameter less than or equal to  $2.5\mu \mathrm{m}$ ), BC (black carbon), OC (organic carbon), and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$ . Only emissions from anthropogenic sources are included in MIX. NMVOC emissions are speciated into model-ready inputs for two chemical mechanisms: CB05 (the Carbon Bond mechanism; Yarwood et al., 2005) and SAPRC-99 (the State Air Pollution Research Center 1999 version; Carter, 2000) (see Tables S1 and S2 in the Supplement). Monthly emissions are provided by sector at  $0.25^{\circ} \times 0.25^{\circ}$  resolution. Gridded emissions are available from http://www.meicmodel.org/dataset-mix. The key features of the MIX inventory are summarized in Table 1.", "This paper documents the methodology and emission datasets of the MIX Asian anthropogenic emission inventory. The regional and national inventories used to develop MIX gridded datasets and the mosaic methodology are presented in Sect. 2. Section 3 presents Asian emissions in 2010 and spatial and temporal variations in emissions. Changes in Asian emissions between 2006 and 2010 are also discussed. Section 4 highlights the major improvements in the new inventory by comparing MIX with other Asian emission in", "NMVOC emissions were speciated to SAPRC-99 and CB05 mechanisms following the explicit species mapping approach documented in Li et al. (2014) (see Fig. 3). Finally, emissions were aggregated to the five MIX sectors and then assembled to monthly emission grid maps over Asia with a uniform spatial resolution of  $0.25^{\circ} \times 0.25^{\circ}$ .", "Based on the mosaic approach and candidate inventories described in Sect. 2, gridded anthropogenic emissions for 10 species were generated over Asia and called the MIX emission inventory. In the MIX inventory, Asian anthropogenic emissions in 2010 are estimated as follows:  $51.3\mathrm{TgSO}_2$ $52.1\mathrm{TgNO}_x$ $336.6\mathrm{TgCO}$ $67.0\mathrm{TgNMVOC}$ $28.8\mathrm{TgNH_3}$ $31.7\mathrm{TgPM}_{10}$ $22.7\mathrm{TgPM}_{2.5}$ $3.5\mathrm{TgBC}$ $8.3\mathrm{TgOC}$ , and  $17.3\mathrm{PgCO}_2$ . Figure 4 presents the emission distributions among sectors over Asia in 2010. Among the different sectors, the industrial sector has the largest contribution to  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  (50% of total), NMVOC (38%),  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  (48%), and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$  (40%) emissions. Power plants have significant contributions for  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  (38% of total),  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  (29%), and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$  (34%) emissions.", "Table 3. National anthropogenic emissions in the MIX emission inventory in 2010 (units: Tg for  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and Gg for other species). Bold values are the total emissions for Asian regions.", "Ratios of different species were widely used to inform emission characteristics. For example,  $\mathrm{SO}_2 / \mathrm{CO}_2$  ratio was used as an indicator of coal combustion and emission control levels (Li et al., 2007), and ratios of  $\mathrm{CO} / \mathrm{CO}_2$  were used to inform combustion efficiency (Wang et al., 2010). Figure 7 compares regional emission ratios of  $\mathrm{SO}_2 / \mathrm{CO}_2$  and  $\mathrm{CO} / \mathrm{CO}_2$  estimated by the MIX inventory. Emission ratios of  $\mathrm{SO}_2 / \mathrm{CO}_2$  are lowest in Other East Asia among different regions, which could be attributed to small share of coal use", "In this work, we also developed Asian emissions for 2006 and 2008 following the same approach of MIX, to illustrate the changes in Asian emissions from 2006 to 2010. Table 5 presents Asian emissions in 2006 and emission ratios of 2010 to 2016 by country. For the whole of Asia, emission growth rates from 2006 to 2010 are estimated as follows:  $-8.1\%$  for  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $+19.2\%$  for  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ ,  $+3.9\%$  for  $\mathrm{CO}$ ,  $+15.5\%$  for NMVOC,  $+1.7\%$  for  $\mathrm{NH}_3$ ,  $-3.4\%$  for  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ ,  $-1.6\%$  for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ,  $+5.5\%$  for BC,  $+1.8\%$  for OC, and  $+19.9\%$  for  $\mathrm{CO}_2$ . Growth in  $\mathrm{CO}_2$  emissions represent the continuously increasing energy use across Asia during 2006-2010, while different trends among species represents differences in the emission control level among sectors and regions. Compared to the increasing emission trends of all species during 2001-2006 (Zhang et al., 2009), the relatively flat or even decreasing emission trends in many species indicate the effectiveness of emission control measures in recent years (Gu et al., 2013; Lin et al., 2010; Wang et al., 2013).", "MIX emission inventory can be accessed publicly from the website of http://www.meicmodel.org/dataset-mix. Both 2008 and 2010 emissions of 10 species with monthly variation at a spatial resolution of  $0.25 \times 0.25^{\circ}$  are available from the website, including  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ , CO,  $\mathrm{NH}_3$ , NMVOC,  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , BC, OC, and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$ . Speciated NMVOC Emissions for CB05 and SAPRC-99 chemical mechanisms are provided at the same spatial and temporal resolution. The MIX in", "with REAS2 and EDGAR v4.2 (EC-JRC/PBL, 2011), two widely used inventories, to highlight the new findings from the mosaic inventory and identify the potential sources of uncertainties. We choose the year of 2008 to conduct the comparison because emissions after 2008 are not available in either REAS2 or EDGAR v4.2. Russian Asia was excluded from comparison. Asian anthropogenic emissions of MIX, REAS2, and EDGAR v4.2 in 2008 are tabulated in Table 7. Over Asia, MIX and REAS differ within  $10\%$  for most species, except for  $\mathrm{NH}_3$  ( $18\%$  higher in REAS),  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  ( $13\%$  higher), and BC ( $13\%$  lower). It is not surprising that the total Asian emission budgets in MIX and REAS2 are simi", "The MIX emission inventory subjects to uncertainties and several limitations. Emission estimates from bottom-up inventories are uncertain due to lack of complete knowledge of human activities and emission from different sources. Uncertainty ranges of an emission inventory could be estimated using propagation of error or Monte Carlo approaches (e.g., Streets et al., 2003; Zhao et al., 2011). However, in a mosaic emission inventory like MIX, a normalized quantitative assessment of uncertainty ranges is difficult because detailed information for emission inventory development is not collected. Table 9 summarized the uncertainty range estimates for China, India, and other Asian regions in different regional emission inventories. It should be noted that those ranges are not directly comparable due to differences in methods (propagation of error or Monte Carlo simulation). However, those numbers might roughly represent the uncertainty ranges in the MIX inventory as it was compiled from several inventories listed in Table 9. In general, uncertainty ranges are relatively small for species which emissions are dominated my large-scale combustion sources (e.g.,  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ , and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$ ) but larger for species whose emissions are mainly from small-scale and scattered sources (e.g., CO, NMVOC, and carbonaceous aerosols). More detailed discussions on the uncertainty sources of Asian emission inventories can be found in previous literatures (e.g., Lu et al., 2011; Zhao et al., 2011; Kurokawa et al., 2013).", "When species in a specific inventory were missing, alternative estimates or datasets were used to fill the gap in which may involve additional uncertainties. In the MIX inventory,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , BC, and OC emissions for Republic of Korea were roughly estimates from  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  emissions in the CAPSS inventory and sector-specific emission ratios between  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  and other aerosol components from Lei et al. (2011). For India, we used ANL-India for  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ , BC, and OC for all sectors and  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  for power plants. REAS2 was used to fill the gap where emissions from ANL-India were absent (see Sect. 2.3 for detailed process procedure). For above cases where estimates of different species in the same country were obtained from", "In this work, we developed a new anthropogenic emission inventory for Asia for the years of 2008 and 2010 by constructing a mosaic of several regional and national emission inventories. MEIC, PKU-NH $_3$ , ANL-India, and CAPSS inventories are used to represent the best available emission data for China, India, and Korea, supplemented with REAS2 to fill gaps. By harmonizing these inventories, monthly emission grids maps for 10 species over Asia were generated for five sectors (power, industry, residential, transportation, and agriculture) at a uniform spatial resolution of  $0.25^{\circ} \times 0.25^{\circ}$ . Gridded speciated NMVOC emissions for SAPRC-99 and CB05 mechanisms were also developed at the same temporal and spatial resolution. This new Asian emission inventory, named MIX, provides model-ready anthropogenic emissions for the MICS-Asia Phase III assessment. The MIX inventory has been also incorporated into the HTAP v2 gridded emission inventory (Janssens-Maenhout et al., 2015) to support the TF HTAP assessment. Gridded emissions are available from the following website: http://www.meicmodel.org/dataset-mix.", "Guan, D., Liu, Z., Geng, Y., Lindner, S., and Hubacek, K.: The gigatonne gap in China's carbon dioxide inventories, Nature Clim. evaluation.extracted_insight: Figure 2’s composite pie‑chart maps reveal that in 2010 the energy and industry sectors dominate emissions of SO₂, NOₓ, CO, and NMVOC, while transport and residential sectors are the largest contributors to PM₂.₅, BC and OC, offering a sector‑level emission portrait for integration into chemical transport models.
evaluation.reason: Visual sector‑specific emission breakdown directly addresses the research theme of source contributions to air quality and points to a high‑value dataset (HTAP v2.2) for further quantitative work.

content.text: The resulting data set, named HTAP_v2.2, is a compilation of annual and monthly grid maps of anthropogenic air pollution emissions (with a  $0.1^{\circ} \times 0.1^{\circ}$  grid resolution). It contains region-specific information on human activity (concerning intensity and geospatial distribution) and on fuel, technology- and process-dependent emission factors and end-of-pipe abatement, but it is not as consistent as a globally consistent emission inventory using international statistics and global geospatial distributions. With the perspective of being used in chemical transport models, this inventory includes the atmospheric gaseous pollutants  $\mathrm{(SO_2, NO_x, CO, NMVOC^4, NH_3)}$  and particulate matter with carbonaceous speciation  $\mathrm{(PM_{10}, PM_{2.5}, BC}$  and OC)5.

content.text: A harmonized global, gridded, air pollution emission data set has been compiled with officially reported, gridded inventories at the national scale, to the extent possible and complemented with science-based inventories for regions and sectors where nationally reported data were not available.

content.text: The HTAP_v2.2 air pollutant grid maps are considered to combine latest available regional information within a complete global data set. The disaggregation by sectors, high spatial and temporal resolution and detailed information on the data sources and references used will provide the user the required transparency. Because HTAP_v2.2 contains primarily official and/or widely used regional emission grid maps, it can be recommended as a global baseline emission inventory, which is regionally accepted as a reference and from which different scenarios assessing emission reduction policies at a global scale could start.

content.text: This paper describes the HTAP global air pollutant reference emission inventory for 2010, which is composed of latest available data from regional inventory compilers. It assures a consistent input for both regional and global modelling as required by the HTAP modelling exercise. The HTAP_v2.2 emission database makes use of consolidated estimates of official and latest available regional information with air pollutant grid maps from US EPA and Environment Canada for North America, EMEP-TNO for Europe, MIX for Asia, and the EDGARv4.3 database for the rest of the world. The mosaic of grid maps provides comprehensive local information on the emission of air pollutants, because it results from the collection of point sources and national emission grid maps at  $0.1^{\circ}$  (for some regions  $0.25^{\circ}$ ) resolution. Even though the HTAP_v2.2 data set is not a self-consistent bottom-up database with activity data of consistent international statistics, with harmonized emission factors, and with global sets of spatial proxy data, it provides a unique set of emission grid maps with global coverage and high spatial resolution, including in particular important point sources. The compilation of implied emission factors and per capita emissions for the different world regions using multiple sources provides the regional and national emission inventory compilers with a valuable asset for comparison with their own data for cross checking and analysis which may lead to identification of future improvement options.

orchestrator_plan.plansummary: 这个检索策略的目标是为“在城市尺度上利用 GIS 与 CFD 做污染 plume 三维体积可视化”提供支撑材料，期望找到能够展示污染 plume 的三维结构、网格化排放数据以及与之配套的数值模型信息。实际检索得到的文献片段大多围绕可视化图形、网格地图和三维模型配置展开：有描述隧道内多污染物浓度剖面的图形（展示浓度随距离的上升趋势），有基于 MIX 亚洲排放清单绘制的高分辨率气体和气溶胶网格图，亦有展示美国 CONUS 区域模型网格和垂直层划分的空间概览。检索到的核心技术细节包括 CMAQ DDM‑3D 的三维配置（12 km 网格、27 层垂直分层、使用 CB6r3 与 AERO6 机制）以及各种气溶胶模型对烟雾沉降过程的验证。虽然其中几条结果是重复或与三维体视化关系不大，但整体提供了 GIS‑based Emission maps、CFD‑style 3‑D atmospheric model setups 以及垂直剖面实验数据等线索，这些都是构建城市污染 plume 三维可视化时可以直接借鉴的关键资源。

content.text: The figure displays grid maps for gaseous and aerosol species based on the MIX Asian emission inventory in 2010, highlighting spatial emission patterns. The maps illustrate improved spatial emission patterns due to the incorporation of high-resolution datasets, assisting in better understanding and management of emissions across Asia.

{"paper_title": "MIX_ a mosaic Asian anthropogenic emission invento", "figure_id": "img_165", "figure_title": "Figure 12. Grid maps for gaseous (a) and aerosol (b) species in the MIX Asian emission inventory, 2010.", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\MIX_ a mosaic Asian anthropogenic emission invento\figure_12.jpg", "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Core Findings", "Showcase Prediction Results"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Geospatial Visualizations", "sub_types": ["Map"]}, "key_entities": ["MIX Asian emission inventory", "gaseous species", "aerosol species", "2010", "CPED", "JEI-DB", "OPRF"], "concise_summary": "The figure displays grid maps for gaseous and aerosol species based on the MIX Asian emission inventory in 2010, highlighting spatial emission patterns.", "inferred_insight": "The maps illustrate improved spatial emission patterns due to the incorporation of high-resolution datasets, assisting in better understanding and management of emissions across Asia.", "references": ["In the MIX inventory, gridded emissions for 10 gaseous and aerosol species were developed at  $0.25 \times 0.25^{\circ}$  resolution. Emission maps of all species in 2010 are shown in Fig. 12. Compared to the previous gridded Asian emission inventories, we believe the spatial patterns are improved because several local high-resolution emission datasets are incorporated, such as CPED for China and JEI-DB and OPRF for Japan. However, for sectors in which emissions are dominated by spatially scattered sources (e.g., residential combustion, solvent use), the spatial distributions in emissions are still uncertain.", "Figure 12.", "Abstract. The MIX inventory is developed for the years 2008 and 2010 to support the Model Inter-Comparison Study for Asia (MICS-Asia) and the Task Force on Hemispheric Transport of Air Pollution (TF HTAP) by a mosaic of up-to-date regional emission inventories. Emissions are estimated for all major anthropogenic sources in 29 countries and regions in Asia. We conducted detailed comparisons of different regional emission inventories and incorporated the best available ones for each region into the mosaic inventory at a uniform spatial and temporal resolution. Emissions are aggregated to five anthropogenic sectors: power, industry, residential, transportation, and agriculture. We estimate the total Asian emissions of 10 species in 2010 as follows:  $51.3\mathrm{TgSO}_2$ ,  $52.1\mathrm{TgNO}_x$ ,  $336.6\mathrm{TgCO}$ ,  $67.0\mathrm{TgNMVOC}$  (non-methane volatile organic compounds),  $28.8\mathrm{TgNH}_3$", "Generally, there are two common approaches to develop a bottom-up emission inventory at regional level. One is using a unified framework of source categories, calculating method, chemical speciation scheme (if applicable), and spatial and temporal allocations (e.g., Streets et al., 2003; Ohara et al., 2007; Lu et al., 2011). Using the unified approach, emissions are estimated in a consistent way with attainable resources. Several Asian emission inventories widely used in the community were developed by the unified approach. Streets et al. (2003) first developed a comprehensive Asian emission inventory for a variety of gaseous and aerosol species for the year 2000 to support the TRACE-P (Transport and Chemical Evolution over the Pacific) campaign (Carmichael et al., 2003), which was subsequently used for MICS-Asia Phase II. Ohara et al. (2007) developed the Regional Emission inventory in Asia (REAS) version 1.1 covering emissions of major species over Asia from 1980 to 2003, which provides estimates of Asian emissions for a long-term period. However, with the unified approach, many region-dependent parameters are shared among different regions due to lack of resources and local knowledge (e.g., emission factors, chemical profiles, spatial proxies, and temporal profiles, etc.), introducing large uncertainties in emission estimates for a specific region (He et al., 2007; Kurokawa et al., 2009).", "The MIX inventory is developed for 2008 and 2010, in accordance with base year simulations in MICS-Asia III and the Task Force on Hemispheric Transport of Air Pollution (TF HTAP). It should be noted that MIX is not comparable to INTEX-B and TRACE-P to derive an emission trend due to differences in methodology and underlying data. In this paper, we also provided Asian emissions for 2006 using the same methodology, partly resolving the problems of trend analysis in mosaic inventories. The gridded MIX emission data for the years 2008 and 2010 are then incorporated", "Figure 1 presents the definition of the MIX domain and emission datasets used for each country and region. The domain of MIX covers 29 countries and regions (the full list of country and region names are listed in Table 1), stretching from Kazakhstan in the west to Russia Far East in the east and from Indonesia in the south to Siberia in the north. Emissions are aggregated into five sectors: power, industry, residential, transportation, and agriculture. Ten chemical species are included in the MIX inventory, including both gaseous and aerosol species:  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ , CO, NMVOC (non-methane volatile organic compounds),  $\mathrm{NH}_3$  (ammonia),  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  (particulate matter with diameter less than or equal to  $10\mu \mathrm{m}$ ),  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  (particulate matter with diameter less than or equal to  $2.5\mu \mathrm{m}$ ), BC (black carbon), OC (organic carbon), and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$ . Only emissions from anthropogenic sources are included in MIX. NMVOC emissions are speciated into model-ready inputs for two chemical mechanisms: CB05 (the Carbon Bond mechanism; Yarwood et al., 2005) and SAPRC-99 (the State Air Pollution Research Center 1999 version; Carter, 2000) (see Tables S1 and S2 in the Supplement). Monthly emissions are provided by sector at  $0.25^{\circ} \times 0.25^{\circ}$  resolution. Gridded emissions are available from http://www.meicmodel.org/dataset-mix. The key features of the MIX inventory are summarized in Table 1.", "This paper documents the methodology and emission datasets of the MIX Asian anthropogenic emission inventory. The regional and national inventories used to develop MIX gridded datasets and the mosaic methodology are presented in Sect. 2. Section 3 presents Asian emissions in 2010 and spatial and temporal variations in emissions. Changes in Asian emissions between 2006 and 2010 are also discussed. Section 4 highlights the major improvements in the new inventory by comparing MIX with other Asian emission in", "NMVOC emissions were speciated to SAPRC-99 and CB05 mechanisms following the explicit species mapping approach documented in Li et al. (2014) (see Fig. 3). Finally, emissions were aggregated to the five MIX sectors and then assembled to monthly emission grid maps over Asia with a uniform spatial resolution of  $0.25^{\circ} \times 0.25^{\circ}$ .", "Based on the mosaic approach and candidate inventories described in Sect. 2, gridded anthropogenic emissions for 10 species were generated over Asia and called the MIX emission inventory. In the MIX inventory, Asian anthropogenic emissions in 2010 are estimated as follows:  $51.3\mathrm{TgSO}_2$ $52.1\mathrm{TgNO}_x$ $336.6\mathrm{TgCO}$ $67.0\mathrm{TgNMVOC}$ $28.8\mathrm{TgNH_3}$ $31.7\mathrm{TgPM}_{10}$ $22.7\mathrm{TgPM}_{2.5}$ $3.5\mathrm{TgBC}$ $8.3\mathrm{TgOC}$ , and  $17.3\mathrm{PgCO}_2$ . Figure 4 presents the emission distributions among sectors over Asia in 2010. Among the different sectors, the industrial sector has the largest contribution to  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  (50% of total), NMVOC (38%),  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  (48%), and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$  (40%) emissions. Power plants have significant contributions for  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  (38% of total),  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  (29%), and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$  (34%) emissions.", "Table 3. National anthropogenic emissions in the MIX emission inventory in 2010 (units: Tg for  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and Gg for other species). Bold values are the total emissions for Asian regions.", "Ratios of different species were widely used to inform emission characteristics. For example,  $\mathrm{SO}_2 / \mathrm{CO}_2$  ratio was used as an indicator of coal combustion and emission control levels (Li et al., 2007), and ratios of  $\mathrm{CO} / \mathrm{CO}_2$  were used to inform combustion efficiency (Wang et al., 2010). Figure 7 compares regional emission ratios of  $\mathrm{SO}_2 / \mathrm{CO}_2$  and  $\mathrm{CO} / \mathrm{CO}_2$  estimated by the MIX inventory. Emission ratios of  $\mathrm{SO}_2 / \mathrm{CO}_2$  are lowest in Other East Asia among different regions, which could be attributed to small share of coal use", "In this work, we also developed Asian emissions for 2006 and 2008 following the same approach of MIX, to illustrate the changes in Asian emissions from 2006 to 2010. Table 5 presents Asian emissions in 2006 and emission ratios of 2010 to 2016 by country. For the whole of Asia, emission growth rates from 2006 to 2010 are estimated as follows:  $-8.1\%$  for  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $+19.2\%$  for  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ ,  $+3.9\%$  for  $\mathrm{CO}$ ,  $+15.5\%$  for NMVOC,  $+1.7\%$  for  $\mathrm{NH}_3$ ,  $-3.4\%$  for  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ ,  $-1.6\%$  for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ,  $+5.5\%$  for BC,  $+1.8\%$  for OC, and  $+19.9\%$  for  $\mathrm{CO}_2$ . Growth in  $\mathrm{CO}_2$  emissions represent the continuously increasing energy use across Asia during 2006-2010, while different trends among species represents differences in the emission control level among sectors and regions. Compared to the increasing emission trends of all species during 2001-2006 (Zhang et al., 2009), the relatively flat or even decreasing emission trends in many species indicate the effectiveness of emission control measures in recent years (Gu et al., 2013; Lin et al., 2010; Wang et al., 2013).", "MIX emission inventory can be accessed publicly from the website of http://www.meicmodel.org/dataset-mix. Both 2008 and 2010 emissions of 10 species with monthly variation at a spatial resolution of  $0.25 \times 0.25^{\circ}$  are available from the website, including  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ , CO,  $\mathrm{NH}_3$ , NMVOC,  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , BC, OC, and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$ . Speciated NMVOC Emissions for CB05 and SAPRC-99 chemical mechanisms are provided at the same spatial and temporal resolution. The MIX in", "with REAS2 and EDGAR v4.2 (EC-JRC/PBL, 2011), two widely used inventories, to highlight the new findings from the mosaic inventory and identify the potential sources of uncertainties. We choose the year of 2008 to conduct the comparison because emissions after 2008 are not available in either REAS2 or EDGAR v4.2. Russian Asia was excluded from comparison. Asian anthropogenic emissions of MIX, REAS2, and EDGAR v4.2 in 2008 are tabulated in Table 7. Over Asia, MIX and REAS differ within  $10\%$  for most species, except for  $\mathrm{NH}_3$  ( $18\%$  higher in REAS),  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  ( $13\%$  higher), and BC ( $13\%$  lower). It is not surprising that the total Asian emission budgets in MIX and REAS2 are simi", "The MIX emission inventory subjects to uncertainties and several limitations. Emission estimates from bottom-up inventories are uncertain due to lack of complete knowledge of human activities and emission from different sources. Uncertainty ranges of an emission inventory could be estimated using propagation of error or Monte Carlo approaches (e.g., Streets et al., 2003; Zhao et al., 2011). However, in a mosaic emission inventory like MIX, a normalized quantitative assessment of uncertainty ranges is difficult because detailed information for emission inventory development is not collected. Table 9 summarized the uncertainty range estimates for China, India, and other Asian regions in different regional emission inventories. It should be noted that those ranges are not directly comparable due to differences in methods (propagation of error or Monte Carlo simulation). However, those numbers might roughly represent the uncertainty ranges in the MIX inventory as it was compiled from several inventories listed in Table 9. In general, uncertainty ranges are relatively small for species which emissions are dominated my large-scale combustion sources (e.g.,  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ , and  $\mathrm{CO}_2$ ) but larger for species whose emissions are mainly from small-scale and scattered sources (e.g., CO, NMVOC, and carbonaceous aerosols). More detailed discussions on the uncertainty sources of Asian emission inventories can be found in previous literatures (e.g., Lu et al., 2011; Zhao et al., 2011; Kurokawa et al., 2013).", "When species in a specific inventory were missing, alternative estimates or datasets were used to fill the gap in which may involve additional uncertainties. In the MIX inventory,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , BC, and OC emissions for Republic of Korea were roughly estimates from  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  emissions in the CAPSS inventory and sector-specific emission ratios between  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  and other aerosol components from Lei et al. (2011). For India, we used ANL-India for  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ , BC, and OC for all sectors and  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  for power plants. REAS2 was used to fill the gap where emissions from ANL-India were absent (see Sect. 2.3 for detailed process procedure). For above cases where estimates of different species in the same country were obtained from", "In this work, we developed a new anthropogenic emission inventory for Asia for the years of 2008 and 2010 by constructing a mosaic of several regional and national emission inventories. MEIC, PKU-NH $_3$ , ANL-India, and CAPSS inventories are used to represent the best available emission data for China, India, and Korea, supplemented with REAS2 to fill gaps. By harmonizing these inventories, monthly emission grids maps for 10 species over Asia were generated for five sectors (power, industry, residential, transportation, and agriculture) at a uniform spatial resolution of  $0.25^{\circ} \times 0.25^{\circ}$ . Gridded speciated NMVOC emissions for SAPRC-99 and CB05 mechanisms were also developed at the same temporal and spatial resolution. This new Asian emission inventory, named MIX, provides model-ready anthropogenic emissions for the MICS-Asia Phase III assessment. The MIX inventory has been also incorporated into the HTAP v2 gridded emission inventory (Janssens-Maenhout et al., 2015) to support the TF HTAP assessment. content.text: The map displays the study domain over the CONUS, highlighting urban, rural regions, and ozonesonde station locations (Boulder and Huntsville). Figure 1 provides a spatial overview of the modeling domain, serving as a reference for understanding spatial distribution and modeling setups for atmospheric analysis.

source_args.query_intent: 3D volumetric visualization of pollutant plumes over urban areas with GIS and CFD modeling

content.text: To compute both atmospheric concentrations of gaseous and particulate pollutants and first-order sensitivity coefficients, required for conducting inverse modeling, we use the Community Multiscale Air Quality model configured with the decoupled direct method in three dimensions (CMAQ DDM-3D) based on CMAQ model version 5.2 (Cohan et al., 2005; Napelenok et al., 2006), developed and released by the EPA. Our study domain covering the CONUS utilizes  $12\mathrm{km}$  grid spacing with  $459\times 299$  grid points (shown in Fig. 1), while the vertical extent between the surface and  $100\mathrm{hPa}$  is discretized with 27 layers of variable thickness. We use carbon bond (CB6r3) for the gas phase and AERO6 for the aerosol chemical mechanism. We obtained the chemical initial and boundary conditions from the CMAQ model with the in-line dust model covering the entire Northern Hemisphere (HCMAQ) (Mathur et al., 2017), providing dynamic chemical lateral boundary conditions in a consistent physical configuration. Details of the HCMAQ simulation and its emission data can be found in Jung et al. (2021) and Vukovich et al. (2018). Since the HCMAQ simulation was based on the year 2016, we applied seasonal scaling for gaseous species based on the comparison

content.text: To compute both atmospheric concentrations of gaseous and particulate pollutants and first-order sensitivity coefficients, required for conducting inverse modeling, we use the Community Multiscale Air Quality model configured with the decoupled direct method in three dimensions (CMAQ DDM-3D) based on CMAQ model version 5.2 (Cohan et al., 2005; Napelenok et al., 2006), developed and released by the EPA. Our study domain covering the CONUS utilizes  $12\mathrm{km}$  grid spacing with  $459\times 299$  grid points (shown in Fig. 1), while the vertical extent between the surface and  $100\mathrm{hPa}$  is discretized with 27 layers of variable thickness. We use carbon bond (CB6r3) for the gas phase and AERO6 for the aerosol chemical mechanism. We obtained the chemical initial and boundary conditions from the CMAQ model with the in-line dust model covering the entire Northern Hemisphere (HCMAQ) (Mathur et al., 2017), providing dynamic chemical lateral boundary conditions in a consistent physical configuration. Details of the HCMAQ simulation and its emission data can be found in Jung et al. (2021) and Vukovich et al. (2018). Since the HCMAQ simulation was based on the year 2016, we applied seasonal scaling for gaseous species based on the comparison

content.text: For the first time, vertical profiles with high temporal and spatial resolution of particulate air pollutants could be recorded by tower elevators (Imhof et al., 2005). Upwind the motorway, no height-dependent difference in the particle number and surface area concentration was observed. On the downwind side, substantially higher concentrations were measured within the plume from near the ground up to  $20\mathrm{m}$  height than above the plume (Fig. 5). This height dependency was found for particles with diameter  $< 300\mathrm{nm}$ , which are soot particles and nucleation mode particles formed by condensation of exhaust gases in most cases and for coarse particles (diameter  $>1\mu \mathrm{m}$ ), originating from tyre and brake abrasion and resuspension of soil dust. Between 300 and  $700\mathrm{nm}$  particle size, no height dependence was found, because motor vehicles emit only a few particles in this size range.

orchestrator_plan.plansummary: 这个策略旨在通过语义检索，寻找能够把空气污染浓度的空间热图与人口密度或社会经济变量叠加展示的资料，从而揭示污染暴露的不平等分布并为更深入的社会层面分析提供视觉切入点。检索结果大多围绕中国主要城市或地区的 PM2.5 等关键污染物的空间分布以及其与人口、二次产业比重、车辆拥有量等社会因素的关联展开。具体而言，检索到的内容包括：展示中国前二十大人口城市年度 PM2.5 热图的图形（结果 1），呈现不同区域（如京津冀、长三角、珠三角等）在不同年份人群暴露程度的比例图（结果 3），以及直接绘制空气质量变化与社会经济因子相关系数的热图（结果 4）。此外，若干文献提供了冬季供暖期间污染物升高的定量表格（结果 2、9），并在讨论中把人口密度、二次产业比重等作为解释变量（结果 10），为后续将污染热图与人口/经济层次结合提供了潜在的数值基线和变量选取线索。总体来看，这些结果既包含了可直接使用的热图素材，也提供了与人口、产业结构等社会经济指标的关联分析框架，为实现“热图叠加人口/社会经济数据”这一可视化目标奠定了数据和方法的双重支撑。

content.text: Table 3 shows the effects of the winter heating period on air quality. The dependent variables are the logarithmic transformations of the different air pollutants; therefore, the estimated coefficient of Heatperiod represents the percentage change of air pollutants. The estimated coefficient of Heatperiod is statistically significant at a  $1\%$  significance level. Specifically, the winter heating policy resulted in increases in AQI,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ ,  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_2$ , and CO by  $26.3\%$ ,  $41.7\%$ ,  $26.1\%$ ,  $19.2\%$ ,  $8.8\%$ , and  $24.4\%$ , respectively, during the winter heating period. Obviously,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  emissions in northern cities during heating periods were most severe. Our empirical findings therefore indicated that winter heating worsened the air quality of northern cities, which was consistent with existing research. However, our estimation results were significantly higher than those of previous studies. For instance, Li and Cao [36] found that  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , CO,  $\mathrm{NO}_2$ , and  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  concentrations increased by  $14.73\%$ ,  $26.79\%$ ,  $20.85\%$ ,  $30.45\%$ , and  $74.10\%$ , respectively, during heating periods based on data from 2013 to 2015. Wang et al. [37] employed RDD to conclude that heating led to increases in AQI by  $10.4\%$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  by  $9.77\%$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  by  $17.25\%$ , CO by  $9.84\%$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_2$  by  $5.23\%$ , and  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  by  $17.1\%$ , respectively. Obviously, many studies have shown that increases in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  are always significantly higher compared to CO and  $\mathrm{NO}_2$  [38,39]. It is noteworthy that these different estimation results can be attributed to the sample data and research design utilized in our study. First, most sample cities were from "TTS" cities, which suffered severe air pollution. Second, the timespan used in our study was less than that in other studies, which could affect calculation results.

content.text: The experimental group consisted of 35 demonstration cities, and these cities were selected for inclusion into the WCHP project. We focused on the selection of the control group containing non-pilot cities during the winter heating period. Referring to Tan et al. [35], Lin and Zhu [18] as the precedents of environmental policy evaluation, we selected control cities that are geographically adjacent to the pilot cities but not been selected in the project. If a city is adjacent to multiple cities, all the adjacent cities will be selected into the control group. Table 1 exhibits the experimental and control cities, and Table 2 summarizes the sample distributions of air pollutant indicators and covariates.

content.text: where  $Heatperiod_{i,n-1}$  is the set of dummy variables, which represents whether the  $i$ -th city is in a heating period at  $n-1$ -day.  $L_n$  represents a series of estimated coefficients 15 days prior to the start time of heating, and  $E_n$  represents a series of estimated coefficients 15 days following the start time of heating. With the omitted time category being the start time of heating,  $L_n$  captures the differences in pollutant indicators between pilot and non-pilot cities relative to differences just prior to the launch of the policy. Here we selected AQI as the air pollutant proxy. Under such specifications, if the signs of the estimated  $Heatperiod_{i,n-1}$  coefficients differ, it can be assumed that changes in pollutant concentrations are not significant.

content.text: As Fig. 4 shows, the 15 day estimator prior to and following the start of heating varied around a value of zero (0) devoid of a clear trend, and most coefficients were not statistically significant. This result indicated a similarity between pilot cities and non-pilot cities regarding AQI. Despite an abnormal increase 4-6 days following the start time of heating, such differences decreased to a negative value (as seen 2 days prior to the start time of heating), and this trend maintained for the ensuing two weeks. These results were consistent with the hypothesis that the WCHP project generated differential trends in pollutant concentrations between pilot and non-pilot cities. It is noteworthy that the period prior to the start time of heating was implemented and continued for 3-4 days in some cities prior to the formal start time of heating, which may have resulted in the early decrease. Additionally, we also estimated the Heatperiod coefficient 30 days following the start time of heating, which also indicated that effects of the WCHP project existed following the start time of heating.

orchestrator_plan.plansummary: 这个策略的出发点是寻找能够以网络图或 Sankey‑式图示方式呈现“源‑至‑受体”空气污染路径的可视化方案，旨在填补当前文献中缺乏直观显示污染物迁移与贡献比例的图形。检索结果主要收集了与大气污染数据可视化相关的图形材料：有展示隧道内多种污染物浓度随距离和时间变化的折线图（如上海东延安路隧道入口‑出口的 NO、NO₂、CO、PM₂.5 时序图），提供源贡献箱线图的 VOC 来源分布（溶剂、燃烧、车辆、汽油蒸发、工业），以及对测量与初始 VOC 剖面的散点回归图，揭示了不同来源之间的线性关联和光化学影响；还有描述监测站点空间布局的示意图和基于 TROPOMI HCHO 检索的观测网络与模型域的地图，进一步展示了卫星‑地面数据的空间分布；最后呈现了 GEOS‑Chem 模型中 NMOG 按化学寿命与挥发度的分布图，突出模型缺口和排放因子来源。整体来看，检索到的内容围绕污染物的时空浓度、来源分解、观测网络布局以及模型覆盖情况提供了多种图形形式，虽不直接是 Sankey 图，但为构建源‑至‑受体可视化提供了丰富的定量与空间线索。

content.text: Visual representation of concentration profiles for multiple pollutants measured at varying distances within a tunnel, showing trends of accumulation and mixing. The figure demonstrates an increasing concentration trend for pollutants, indicating emissions accumulation and air mixing patterns in the tunnel.
